title: Warehouse Fleet Polarity Array with Pre-Wired Dock Cells
strategies: trivial: emit the first non-singular seeded random +/-1 completion that respects the pre-wired cells (nonzero |det|, ~0.1)
greedy: seeded multi-restart random sampling, keep the completion with the largest exact |det| (beats trivial, no local search)

strong: Sherman-Morrison rank-1 incremental hill climbing on |det| over the free (non-wired) cells with many random restarts -- flips the free sign maximizing |1 + delta * Ainv[j][i]| until a local optimum
invalid: emit entries outside {-1,+1} (0s) or violate a pre-wired cell -> feasibility gate rejects -> 0
notes: Odd N (9..23) => no Hadamard/Paley matrix exists at these orders, and the pre-wired cells forbid dropping in any known closed-form max-det construction, so the optimum stays genuinely unknown. Determinant scored EXACTLY via Bareiss integer elimination; objective is bit-length of |det| (compresses the super-exponential range so the score never trivially saturates).
